english: (1) structural particle for verb complements; (2) to get, obtain; (3) must, have to

etymology.description: 貝(貝) (bèi, 'cowry') + 又(又) (yòu, 'hand') -> obtaining valuables -> 彳(彳) (chì, 'step') added, 又 changed to 寸(寸) (cùn, 'inch'), 貝 corrupted to 旦(旦) (dàn, 'dawn') -> final form
etymology.interpretation: The character originated as a depiction of a hand picking up a cowry, symbolizing 'obtaining' valuables. The 彳(彳) (chì, 'step') was added to suggest movement on a road, enhancing the idea of acquisition. Over time, script evolutions saw substitute elements: the component 又(又) (yòu, 'hand') changed to 寸(寸) (cùn, 'inch'), reflecting the Qin script's tendency, and 貝(貝) (bèi, 'cowry') was simplified to 旦(旦) (dàn, 'dawn'). These changes led to the character's modern form. This transition mirrors the evolved meanings in usage: 'to get', from obtaining something physically; 'must', representing the necessity of movement or action; and its role as a structural particle in grammar for completion or necessity in expressions.
etymology.simplification: In simplified form, 得 remains unchanged from its traditional representation.

examples.chinese: 他跑得很快(他跑得很快)。
我得去工作(我得去工作)。
examples.english: I have to go to work.

examples.chinese: 她昨天赢得了比赛(她昨天贏得了比賽)。
